raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2025, I used my Citibank credit card to pay {$36.00} for an XXXX ride. After the trip, I received a receipt that was not in English. I contacted XXXX multiple times through their in-app support and official help site to request an English version of the receipt. Although they instructed me to change my language settings, doing so did not affect previously issued receipts. I made it clear that I had already updated both my device and account language settings, but the receipt remained unchanged. 

XXXX repeatedly responded with generic replies and falsely stated that the issue had been resolved, but they never provided the requested English version of the receipt.

I then filed a dispute with Citibank, explaining that while the ride itself occurred, the service was incomplete due to the merchant 's refusal to deliver appropriate documentation in English. Citibank denied the dispute, stating only that the service was rendered, without addressing the issue of the missing documentation or investigating the

merchants failure to respond properly. Citibanks investigation was incomplete and relied solely on the merchants misleading statement. As a result, I was rebilled for a service that was not fully delivered. I am requesting that Citibank re-investigate the case and consider the failure to provide essential documentation as a material deficiency in the service.
